content.problemStatement:
TaskGiven an array A and an integer k. Print the Kth Largest element from the array.

A Heap has already been implemented for your convenience.


Input Format

The first line of input contains two integers NNN and KKK, denoting the number of elements in the array and the integer K.
Next line contains NNN integers of the array.


Output Format
In a single line output the Kth Largest element in the array
content.inputFormat: The first line of input contains two integers N and K, denoting the number of elements in the array and the integer K. The next line contains N integers of the array.
content.outputFormat: The Kth Largest element in the array.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The array is [1, 78, 29, 48, 19, 2, 17, 134, 93, 13]. The 4th largest element is 48.
The array is [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]. The 2nd largest element is 4.
The array is [10, 20, 30]. The largest element is 30.

solution.approach: To find the Kth largest element in the array, we can use a max heap. We first build a max heap from the given array. Then, we extract the maximum element from the heap K-1 times. The element left at the root of the heap after K-1 extractions is the Kth largest element.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) — building the heap takes O(N log N) time, and extracting the maximum element K-1 times takes O(K log N) time. Since K <= N, the overall time complexity is O(N log N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — we need to store the array elements in the heap.
